# `.sacxsinusprecautions` — Sinus Precautions

> [!tip] Usage
> Add to post-op instructions or plan section for any case with OAC risk or confirmed sinus involvement (maxillary extractions, sinus lifts, Le Fort osteotomies).

---

Sinus precautions:
- No nose blowing or forceful sniffing for ___ weeks.
- Sneeze or cough with the mouth open; do not pinch or occlude the nostrils.
- Avoid positive- or negative-pressure activities including spitting, sucking through straws, smoking or vaping, CPAP use, scuba diving, and playing wind or brass instruments until cleared.
- No flying for ___ days.
- Do not rinse vigorously or use oral irrigators (e.g., Waterpik).
- Limit bending at the waist, heavy lifting, and strenuous exercise to minimize sinus pressure changes.
- Elevate head with an extra pillow while sleeping for the first ___ days.
- If OTC decongestant is recommended: pseudoephedrine ___ mg PO q___ h or oxymetazoline nasal spray BID x 3 days (do not exceed 3 days to avoid rebound congestion).
- If you notice air or fluid passing between your mouth and nose, or experience a salty or foul taste, contact the OMFS clinic immediately.
- Seek emergency care for: uncontrolled bleeding, progressive facial swelling with difficulty breathing or swallowing, or fever >101.5°F.
